sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Village Super Market, Inc. Declares Quarterly Cash Dividends for Class A and Class B Shares
feed.summary: Village Super Market, Inc. announced its Board of Directors has declared quarterly cash dividends of $0.25 per Class A common share and $0.1625 per Class B common share. These dividends are payable on April 23, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 2, 2026. The company operates 34 supermarkets and three specialty markets in New York City, and saw a 6.89% revenue increase in Q2 2026, reaching $641 million.

feed.title: Activist Starboard is said to build $350 million stake in CarMax
feed.summary: Activist investor Starboard Value LP has reportedly built a $350 million stake in CarMax Inc., the used-car retailer. This move suggests that Starboard may push for changes or better performance at the company. The specific intentions of the activist fund are not yet clear.

feed.title: Henrietta Lacks family settles lawsuit against Viatris over use of her cells
feed.summary: The family of Henrietta Lacks has settled their lawsuit against generic drugmaker Viatris, resolving another legal dispute concerning the unauthorized use of her cells. This settlement follows previous agreements with Novartis and Thermo Fisher, as the family continues to seek justice for Lacks' invaluable contribution to medical research without her consent. Lacks' cells, known as HeLa cells, were taken in 1951 without her knowledge and have since become a cornerstone of scientific advancement.

feed.title: Invesco Bond Fund (NYSE:VBF) Plans $0.07 Monthly Dividend
feed.summary: Invesco Bond Fund (NYSE:VBF) has announced a monthly dividend of $0.0665 per share, which annualizes to a 5.3% yield. Shareholders of record by March 16th will receive this payment on March 31st, with the ex-dividend date also set for March 16th. VBF is an actively managed ETF focusing on investment-grade fixed-income securities with an intermediate duration strategy.

feed.title: Health100: CVS Health's AI-Powered Platform Reshaping Patient Engagement in 2026
feed.summary: CVS Health has launched Health100, an AI-powered health engagement platform developed in partnership with Google Cloud, aimed at personalizing patient experiences across its pharmacies, clinics, and digital services. This initiative, rolled out in early 2026, seeks to enhance patient interaction and operational efficiency, leveraging AI to offer tailored health recommendations, appointment scheduling, and virtual consultations. Health100 is positioned to drive revenue growth and efficiency for CVS Health, with potential to increase same-store sales and Medicare Advantage enrollment, despite ongoing market challenges and investor caution.

feed.title: A.O. Smith Corp. Stock (ISIN: US0003711006) Eyes Industrial Recovery as Water-Treatment Demand Strengthens
feed.summary: A.O. Smith Corp. is strategically repositioning its business towards higher-margin water-treatment solutions and commercial systems, moving away from commodity water heaters. This shift is anticipated to drive margin expansion and leverage increased demand in water infrastructure and environmental treatment markets, particularly in North America, Asia-Pacific, and Europe. The company's strong balance sheet and focus on organic growth and targeted acquisitions support a stable dividend and attractive long-term returns for investors.

feed.title: Lululemon Founder Proxy Fight: Battle Over Innovation & Brand Future | 2026 - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Lululemon's founder has escalated a proxy fight, criticizing the company for losing its innovative edge and becoming a slow-moving entity. He aims to replace several directors at the upcoming annual meeting, asserting the campaign is about brand direction, not his return to leadership. The company maintains it has adapted and led the industry despite the lack of founder involvement over the past decade.

feed.title: Should Medtronic’s (MDT) Strong Q3 and 48-Year Dividend Streak Prompt Portfolio Reconsideration?
feed.summary: Medtronic (MDT) reported stronger-than-expected Q3 2026 results, featuring 6% year-over-year organic revenue growth and a reaffirmed US$0.71 dividend, extending its 48-year streak of increases. This performance reinforces its position as an income-focused healthcare company with robust cash generation, though execution risk in new product ramp-ups remains a key investor concern. The article also highlights an estimated fair value of $111.69, suggesting a potential 28% upside.

feed.title: A Look At Expeditors (EXPD) Valuation After New US$3b Buyback And Earnings Beat
feed.summary: Expeditors International of Washington (EXPD) is gaining attention due to a new US$3 billion share repurchase program and Q4 2025 earnings exceeding forecasts. Despite these positive developments, the stock's valuation is complex, with a P/E ratio of 23.5x appearing overvalued compared to its fair ratio and the global logistics industry average, yet undervalued according to a discounted cash flow model. Investors need to weigh these conflicting signals to determine if a buying opportunity exists a-or if future growth is already priced in.

feed.title: Synchrony Financial (SYF) Valuation Revisited After Recent Share Price Pullback
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial (SYF) has experienced a recent share price pullback, with declines over the past month and three months, despite strong longer-term returns. Simply Wall St's analysis suggests the stock is significantly undervalued, with an estimated intrinsic value of $145 per share, far exceeding the current market price and consensus analyst targets. This potential undervaluation is attributed to factors like strong excess returns and sustained profitability, although risks like credit losses or new regulations could challenge this narrative.

feed.title: Musk says Tesla's 'gigantic' chip fab project to launch in seven days
feed.summary: Elon Musk announced that Tesla's "Terafab" project, a colossal initiative to manufacture artificial intelligence chips, will commence in seven days. This project aims to address the anticipated shortage of AI chips needed for Tesla's autonomous driving ambitions, as existing supplier capacities are deemed insufficient. Musk previously hinted at potential collaborations with Intel, TSMC, and Samsung for this endeavor.

feed.title: Wingstop’s Q4 Earnings Boost Confidence, Shares Surge
feed.summary: Wingstop's Q4 earnings significantly surpassed expectations, with an adjusted EPS of $1.00 against an anticipated $0.83, leading to a 4.86% stock surge. The company's robust performance is attributed to strong sales growth, strategic global expansion, and the successful rollout of Smart Kitchens, which have fueled analyst optimism and increased price targets. Despite a high P/E ratio, Wingstop's operational efficiency and growth trajectory position it well for continued expansion in the consumer discretionary sector.

feed.title: Thomson Reuters Files Documents for Proposed Return of Capital and Share Consolidation Transactions
feed.summary: Thomson Reuters has filed its management proxy circular and related documents for proposed return of capital and share consolidation transactions. These transactions involve a special cash distribution of US$605 million followed by a share consolidation. Shareholders will vote on these proposals at a special meeting on April 28, 2026.

feed.title: UR-Energy: Strengthening Cost Profile, Expanding Resources, and Contracted Sales Support Buy Rating and $2.50 Target
feed.summary: Maxim Group analyst Tate Sullivan has maintained a Buy rating on UR-Energy (URG) stock with a $2.50 price target, citing the company's improved cost profile, expanding resources at Lost Creek, and imminent startup of the Shirley Basin project. Sullivan emphasizes the company's financial resilience, growing contracted sales, and a strong balance sheet supported by capital raises. H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating with a $2.30 price target, highlighting the supportive demand for uranium.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley says Fed risks are skewed towards later and more cuts
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley suggests that the Federal Reserve's policy path is likely to involve rate cuts occurring later than anticipated, but potentially being larger in magnitude. The firm expects core inflation to slow from Q2 despite strong Q1 readings, due to fading tariff impacts and slowing shelter inflation. Consequently, Morgan Stanley foresees two rate cuts this year, with clearer evidence of disinflation needed before the easing cycle begins.

feed.title: Wellington Management Boosts Stake in Intercontinental Exchange
feed.summary: Wellington Management Group LLP significantly increased its stake in Intercontinental Exchange Inc. (NYSE:ICE) during the third quarter of 2025, adding over 4 million shares. This 64.7% increase brings their total ownership to over 10.3 million shares, valued at approximately $1.74 billion. The move reflects Wellington Management's confidence in the financial services provider's long-term growth prospects and is expected to influence market sentiment and ICE's stock performance.

feed.title: Wealth Advisors of Tampa Bay Boosts Hasbro Stake
feed.summary: Wealth Advisors of Tampa Bay LLC has increased its stake in Hasbro, Inc. by 13.7% in the third quarter of 2026, now owning 54,797 shares valued at $4.16 million. This move suggests the firm sees growth potential in Hasbro, a major player in the global toy and entertainment industry with iconic brands. The acquisition of an additional 6,620 shares brings their total holdings to a significant investment, indicating long-term confidence in the company.

feed.title: Apple Reduces App Store Fees in China Amid Regulatory Talks
feed.summary: Apple is reducing its App Store commission rates in China, effective March 15, 2026, lowering the standard rate from 30% to 25% and the small business program rate from 15% to 12%. This move comes after discussions with Chinese regulators, who investigated Apple's app fees, and follows similar actions by Google. The fee reduction aims to align rates with other global markets, potentially saving Chinese developers over 6 billion yuan annually while also signaling Apple's strategy to maintain developer loyalty and market share amidst growing regulatory and competitive pressures.

feed.title: Leidos Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:LDOS) Declares $0.43 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:LDOS) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.43 per share, payable on March 31st to shareholders of record as of March 16th. This dividend implies a 1.0% yield and an annualized payout of $1.72, which is well-covered by the company's earnings with a low payout ratio of 14.8%. The company has consistently raised its dividend for six consecutive years and analysts project strong future EPS, indicating the sustainability of its dividend payments.

feed.title: Salesforce (CRM) Maintains Outperform Rating Amid Growth Prospec
feed.summary: BNP Paribas has reiterated its Outperform rating on Salesforce (CRM), anticipating revenue growth acceleration in the latter half of the year, possibly by Q3. Salesforce is focusing on strategic initiatives like cost management, targeted acquisitions, and share buybacks, while leveraging its customer data and AI capabilities to maintain a competitive edge despite concerns about agentic AI systems. The company shows consistent revenue growth, strong profitability, and attractive valuation metrics, though liquidity constraints and market volatility remain key considerations for investors.

feed.title: WXM Stock News Today | Earnings, Events & Price Alerts
feed.summary: This page provides news and event updates for WXM stock, focusing on WF International. Recent news includes WF International receiving a Nasdaq notification for non-compliance with the minimum bid price, securing distribution rights for Hisense HVAC in China, and obtaining HITACHI distribution rights for advanced HVAC systems. The company also announced the pricing of a follow-on public offering.

feed.title: Weighing Vermilion Energy (TSX:VET) Valuation After A Strong Multi‑Month Share Price Run
feed.summary: Vermilion Energy (TSX:VET) has experienced a significant share price increase of 13% over the past month and 36% over the last three months, reaching CA$16.16. Despite this strong performance, the company is considered approximately 5% overvalued compared to its fair value estimate of CA$15.41, based on updated financial models. However, its P/S ratio of 1.4x is below the industry average, suggesting potential value while also highlighting ongoing concerns about its debt and past losses.

feed.title: Carvana, Warner Bros, Axon, Energy Fuels, Sarepta Insider Shakeup
feed.summary: This article details recent insider trading activities across five companies: Carvana (CVNA), Warner Bros (WBD), Axon Enterprise (AXON), Energy Fuels (UUUU), and Sarepta Therapeutics (SRPT). Insiders at these companies have sold significant amounts of shares, signaling potential concerns about valuation or profit-taking. Noteworthy sales include J Danforth Quayle selling Carvana shares, Priya Aiyar and Bruce Campbell selling Warner Bros stock, Joshua Isner cashing out a large sum from Axon, and directors at Energy Fuels and Sarepta Therapeutics also reducing their stakes.

feed.title: Thematics Asset Management Lowers Stake in Wabtec $WAB
feed.summary: Thematics Asset Management reduced its stake in Wabtec (NYSE:WAB) by 22.4% in Q3, selling 27,187 shares and now owning 94,141 shares valued at approximately $18.9 million. This adjustment comes as Wabtec reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates, raising its FY-2026 EPS guidance, and announcing a dividend increase and a $1.2 billion share buyback program. Despite insider selling, analyst sentiment remains positive with a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and a $269 average price target.

feed.title: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Cooling
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) shares have recently seen a cooling in price performance, despite positive longer-term returns, and are currently trading below their estimated intrinsic value. The most popular narrative suggests a 16.6% undervaluation, with a fair value of $893.88, driven by the company's diversified commercial engine, success of Dupixent and Eylea HD, and a "soft landing" anticipated for Eylea biosimilars. Investors are encouraged to conduct further research into the underlying forecasts and risks.

feed.title: Interval Partners LP Boosts Rockwell Automation Stake by 31%

Rockwell Automation is a global leader in providing industrial automation and digital transformation solutions.

feed.title: Is Iron Mountain (IRM) Still Attractive After 28% One Year Share Price Gain
feed.summary: Iron Mountain (IRM) has seen a 28% share price gain over the past year, prompting an analysis of its current valuation. Using Discounted Cash Flow and Price-to-Sales analyses, Simply Wall St suggests the stock is currently undervalued. The article also presents bullish and bearish narratives to help investors consider different potential future values for the company, highlighting its hybrid role in traditional records and digital infrastructure.

feed.title: How Investors May Respond To Jack Henry (JKHY) Securing ICBA Endorsement For Its AI Fraud Platform
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates recently secured an ICBA endorsement for its AI-driven Financial Crimes Defender platform, bolstering its position in the community banking sector. While this reinforces the company's commitment to modernizing banking infrastructure and AI-enabled compliance tools, investors should consider it against existing expectations of modest revenue and earnings growth. The endorsement supports Jack Henry's narrative for future revenue and earnings, but competition from agile fintech platforms remains a key risk.

feed.title: CNH Stock Falls -14% In 9-Day Spree On Citigroup Target Cut
feed.summary: CNH stock experienced a 14% decline over a 9-day period. This drop was primarily influenced by Citigroup's decision to cut its price target for the company. The article title highlights the significant impact of analyst ratings on stock performance.

feed.title: Amgen Inc. Stock (ISIN: US0311621009) Gains Defensive Appeal Amid Leadership Shift and Biotech Resilience
feed.summary: Amgen Inc. stock (US0311621009) is showing defensive strength, closing at $366.21 on March 13, 2026, and is up nearly 12% year-to-date, attracting investors seeking stable biotech names. The company's resilience is further bolstered by the appointment of a new CFO, Olivier Leonetti, and a strategic partnership with TrumpRx, which are expected to drive efficiency and market access. These developments, along with robust financial projections and a strong dividend yield, position Amgen as an attractive holding for diversified portfolios, particularly in Europe.

feed.title: How Strong Q4 Results And Upbeat 2026 Guidance At Republic Services (RSG) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Republic Services (RSG) reported strong Q4 2025 results and provided optimistic 2026 guidance, leading to positive analyst ratings and increased institutional investment. This reinforces the company's long-term business model rooted in essential waste services and expansion into higher-margin environmental solutions. Despite underlying execution risks linked to acquisitions and capital programs, analysts project a 9% upside to its current price, with target revenues of $19.3 billion and earnings of $2.7 billion by 2028.

feed.title: Ray J Hit With New American Express Lawsuit Over Alleged $78K Credit Card Debt
feed.summary: R&B singer Ray J, legally known as Ray Norwood, is being sued by American Express for an alleged unpaid credit card balance of over $78,000. American Express claims Norwood failed to repay $78,704.56, breaching a cardholder agreement and is seeking the full balance plus interest and legal costs. This new lawsuit follows a separate December action by American Express against Ray J and Tronix Network for an alleged $139,849.85 debt.

feed.title: Omnicom Media appoints Bradley Rogers as CEO of OMD USA
feed.summary: Omnicom Media has appointed Bradley Rogers as the new chief executive officer of OMD Worldwide’s US business, effective March 23. Rogers brings over 25 years of experience in media, creative services, and digital platforms, having held leadership roles at various global organizations. He succeeds Chrissie Hanson, who is stepping down after four years.

feed.title: Thematics Asset Management Has $17.60 Million Position in IDEX Corporation $IEX
feed.summary: Thematics Asset Management significantly increased its stake in IDEX Corporation (NYSE:IEX) during the third quarter, now holding 108,154 shares valued at $17.60 million. This comes as institutional investors collectively own nearly 98% of the company's stock. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for IDEX with an average price target of $228.22, following the company's Q4 earnings beat and strong FY2026 guidance.

feed.title: Bernstein SocGen cuts Humana stock price target on Stars pressure
feed.summary: Bernstein SocGen Group has reduced its price target for Humana Inc. (NYSE:HUM) from $344 to $211, while maintaining an Outperform rating, due to pressures from Medicare Advantage Stars ratings and increased medical loss ratio risks. Despite the challenging outlook and downward earnings revisions from analysts, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued. Humana recently completed a $1 billion public offering of junior subordinated notes and is identified by Wolfe Research as highly vulnerable to shareholder engagement.

feed.title: Tyler Technologies, Inc. $TYL Stake Trimmed by Thematics Asset Management
feed.summary: Thematics Asset Management recently trimmed its stake in Tyler Technologies (NYSE:TYL) by 9.8% in Q3, reducing its holding to 35,170 shares worth $18.4 million. Despite this, institutional ownership remains high at 93.3%, with Wellington Management significantly boosting its stake. The company missed Q3 earnings expectations, reporting an EPS of $2.64 versus $2.71 expected and revenue of $575.2 million against $591.1 million anticipated, but maintains a consensus "Moderate Buy" analyst rating with a target price of $467.54.

feed.title: Kraft Heinz and the cost of narrow capitalism
feed.summary: The article argues that Kraft Heinz's struggles stem from a focus on financial engineering and cost-cutting rather than investment in innovation, sustainability, and brand development. Author Paul Polman, former CEO of Unilever, reflects on Kraft Heinz's failed takeover bid for Unilever and asserts that short-term, shareholder-primacy approaches ultimately destroy long-term value, serving as a warning for other companies. He contends that businesses must align with societal shifts towards healthier products and purpose-led brands to ensure lasting success.

feed.title: COO Stock Falls -17% With A 9-day Losing Streak On Revenue Shock
feed.summary: The article reports that the COO stock has experienced a significant -17% drop, extending its losing streak to nine consecutive days. This sharp decline is attributed to a "revenue shock" that has impacted the company's financial performance.

feed.title: Equifax Inc Stock (ISIN: US2946001011) Faces Headwinds Amid Data Security Scrutiny and Economic Unce
feed.summary: Equifax Inc. stock is currently facing challenges due to ongoing data security concerns, regulatory pressures, and a slowing U.S. consumer lending market. These factors impact its core revenue drivers, such as mortgage and auto loan originations, and are requiring increased investment in compliance and technology. Despite these headwinds, the company's resilient Workforce Solutions segment and international operations offer some stability, while investors monitor potential catalysts like Fed rate cuts and new AI product launches.
